name: Hengrui Medicine
shortDescription: State-origin generics maker that pivoted to oncology innovation and is now China's largest pharma by market cap
narrativeHook: Hengrui is the bridge between two eras of Chinese pharma: a 1970s state factory in Lianyungang that turned itself into the country's largest innovator, then proved the model works abroad with a $14B+ in announced license-out value to Merck KGaA, GSK, and IDEAYA in 2024-2025.

leadAsset.name: Camrelizumab (AiRuiKa)
leadAsset.indication: Hepatocellular carcinoma, NSCLC, esophageal SCC, nasopharyngeal carcinoma, classical Hodgkin's lymphoma
leadAsset.status: Approved in China across 8 indications; FDA NDA (with rivoceranib) under review with PDUFA target July 23, 2026 after two CRLs

timeline.status: Founded 1970 as the state-owned Lianyungang Pharmaceutical Factory in Jiangsu
timeline.note: Origin as a small state factory making basic medicines

timeline.status: Sun Piaoyang takes over as director in 1990; pivots to oncology generics, factory restructured as Jiangsu Hengrui Pharmaceuticals in 1997
timeline.note: Revenue passes RMB100M in 1996 on strength of cancer drug portfolio

timeline.status: Shanghai Stock Exchange IPO Oct 18, 2000 (ticker 600276); becomes a leading domestic generics and oncology player through the 2000s
Apatinib (Aitan, VEGFR2 TKI) — Hengrui's first internally-discovered novel drug — approved by NMPA in 2014 for advanced gastric cancer
timeline.note: Marks transition from generics-led to innovator pipeline

timeline.status: Pyrotinib (HER2 TKI) approved 2018; ramps R&D spend ahead of NMPA reforms and ICH harmonisation
Camrelizumab (PD-1) approved by NMPA May 2019 — third domestic PD-1 to clear the China market — for relapsed/refractory classical Hodgkin's lymphoma
timeline.note: Becomes Hengrui's flagship asset and template for combination regimens

timeline.status: Centralised volume-based procurement crushes Hengrui's generics business; revenue and profit fall in 2021-2022 as the company doubles down on innovative drugs
timeline.note: Sun Piaoyang steps down as chair in Jan 2020; daughter Sun Yuanhua and CEO Dai Hongbin lead the innovation pivot

timeline.status: FY2024 revenue RMB27.99B (+22.6%) and net profit RMB6.34B (+47.3%); R&D RMB8.23B (29.4% of sales); IDEAYA in-licenses DLL3 ADC SHR-4849 for up to $1.045B (Dec 2024)
timeline.note: Camrelizumab + rivoceranib first-line HCC NDA under FDA review (initial CRL May 2024)

timeline.status: HKEX dual listing May 23, 2025 (ticker 1276) — HK$11.4B raised, largest HK pharma IPO in 5 years; Merck KGaA licenses HRS-5346 Lp(a) inhibitor (Mar 2025, $200M upfront / up to $1.97B); GSK $500M-upfront / up-to-$12B 12-program deal (Jul 2025); FY2025 revenue RMB31.63B (+13%), net profit RMB7.72B (+21.8%)
timeline.note: Innovative-drug sales reach 58% of drug revenue in FY2025; market cap ~$55B; second FDA CRL on camrelizumab/rivoceranib Mar 2025; resubmitted with PDUFA July 23, 2026

milestones.label: Founded as Lianyungang Pharmaceutical Factory
Sun Piaoyang appointed director
Restructured as Jiangsu Hengrui Pharmaceuticals
Shanghai Stock Exchange IPO (600276)
Apatinib (first novel drug) approved by NMPA
Camrelizumab (PD-1) approved by NMPA
Sun Piaoyang steps down as chair
IDEAYA in-licenses DLL3 ADC SHR-4849 (up to $1.045B)
Hong Kong dual listing (HKEX: 1276) raises HK$11.4B
Merck KGaA licenses HRS-5346 Lp(a) inhibitor (up to ~$2B)
GSK 12-program deal: $500M upfront, up to $12B in milestones

name: 3SBio
shortDescription: Shenyang-based biologics pioneer behind China's dominant EPO and TPO franchises; vaulted into global headlines with the 2025 Pfizer PD-1/VEGF bispecific deal
narrativeHook: Founded as Shenyang Sunshine in 1993 to make recombinant biologics affordable, 3SBio spent two decades quietly leading China's EPO and TPO markets — then in May 2025 turned its in-house PD-1/VEGF bispecific (SSGJ-707) into a $1.25B-upfront, up-to-$6B Pfizer deal, the largest single-asset license-out by a Chinese biotech to that point.

leadAsset.name: TPIAO (rhTPO)
leadAsset.indication: Chemotherapy-induced thrombocytopenia, ITP
leadAsset.status: World's only commercial rhTPO; RMB 5.06B FY2024 revenue (+20.4% YoY)

timeline.status: Founded as Shenyang Sunshine Pharmaceutical by Major General Lou Dan in 1993
timeline.note: Built around making interferon and other recombinant proteins affordable in China

timeline.status: Launched EPIAO (1998) and TPIAO (2006); NASDAQ IPO Feb 2007 as 'SSRX'
timeline.note: Co-founder Lou Jing joined as R&D director in 1995; became chairman in 2012

timeline.status: Taken private from NASDAQ in 2013 by Lou Jing and CITIC PE for ~$370M
Hong Kong IPO June 11, 2015 at HK$9.10/share, raising ~$710.7M (HKEX: 1530)
Inetetamab (Cipterbin), in-house anti-HER2 mAb, NMPA-approved June 2020
Pipeline expansion to ~30 candidates; SSGJ-707 (PD-1/VEGF bispecific) advances on CLF2 platform
Pfizer license-out for SSGJ-707 announced May 2025, completed July 24, 2025: $1.25B upfront + up to $4.8B milestones + $100M equity; Phase 3 begins in China and US

milestones.label: Founded as Shenyang Sunshine Pharmaceutical
EPIAO (rhEPO) approved in China
TPIAO (rhTPO) approved — world's first commercial rhTPO
NASDAQ IPO under ticker SSRX
Taken private from NASDAQ for ~$370M
Hong Kong IPO (HKEX: 1530), raising ~$710.7M
Inetetamab (Cipterbin), self-developed anti-HER2 mAb, NMPA-approved
Pfizer SSGJ-707 deal: $1.25B upfront, up to $6B total

name: BGI Group
founders: Wang Jian, Yang Huanming, Liu Siqi, Yu Jun
shortDescription: China's flagship genomics platform — sequencing infrastructure, NIPT, and the DNBSEQ instrument line — split across BGI Genomics (services) and MGI Tech (instruments), and a recurring named target in US biosecurity legislation
narrativeHook: Founded in 1999 to put China inside the Human Genome Project, BGI grew into the only non-Western company building its own clinical sequencing instruments at scale — and became the first Chinese genomics name written by name into US biosecurity law.

leadAsset.name: DNBSEQ sequencer line (MGI Tech)
leadAsset.indication: Clinical and research genomics; NIFTY NIPT; oncology and reproductive testing
leadAsset.status: 1,270 new sequencers installed in 2024 (+49% YoY); ~55% share of China sequencer tenders in 9M 2024

timeline.status: Founded September 1999 in Beijing as the Beijing Genomics Institute by Wang Jian, Yang Huanming, Liu Siqi and Yu Jun to deliver China's 1% share of the Human Genome Project; relocated headquarters to Shenzhen in 2007
timeline.note: Operates as a non-governmental research institute backed in part by Wang Jian's prior venture Jubilee Biotechnology

timeline.status: Bought 128 Illumina HiSeq 2000 instruments (2010) to anchor the world's largest sequencing center; launched NIFTY NIPT clinically in 2011; opened BGI Americas (Cambridge MA) and BGI Europe (Copenhagen)
timeline.note: Established the global services scale that justified building proprietary instruments

timeline.status: Acquired Complete Genomics (Mountain View, CA) for US$117.6M in March 2013 after CFIUS clearance, gaining the DNB nanoball sequencing IP that became the DNBSEQ line; spun out MGI Tech as a sequencing-instrument subsidiary in 2016
timeline.note: First Chinese acquisition of a US sequencing-technology company

timeline.status: BGI Genomics services arm IPO'd on Shenzhen ChiNext July 14, 2017 (300676), raising RMB ~547M (~US$81M); jumped 44% on debut

Mammoth Foundation (BGI-sponsored Shenzhen non-profit) deployed COVID-19 testing labs and donated kits across Europe, the Middle East and Africa; BGI Genomics revenue spiked on global PCR/sequencing demand

MGI Tech IPO'd on Shanghai STAR Market September 2022 (688114), raising RMB ~3.6B (~US$510M) to scale DNBSEQ instrument manufacturing and overseas sales after a 2019 patent settlement opened markets outside China
Named alongside MGI and Complete Genomics in the BIOSECURE Act (H.R.7085), which passed the US House 306–81 on Sept 9, 2024 but failed to make the FY2025 NDAA
timeline.note: MGI's 9M 2024 share of China sequencer tenders reached ~55%, displacing Illumina in the home market

timeline.status: BIOSECURE Act enacted as Section 851 of the FY2026 NDAA, signed Dec 18, 2025; BGI Genomics FY2024 revenue RMB 3.87B (~US$531M, -11% YoY) with a RMB 902.7M net loss; MGI Tech FY2024 revenue RMB 3.01B (~US$414M, +3% YoY) with 1,270 new sequencer installs
timeline.note: Final law no longer names specific entities; OMB has up to 12 months to publish the official Biotechnology Companies of Concern list — BGI Group, BGI Genomics, MGI Tech and Forensic Genomics International remain on the underlying 1260H list

milestones.label: Founded as Beijing Genomics Institute to join the Human Genome Project
Relocated headquarters to Shenzhen
NIFTY NIPT launched clinically
Acquired Complete Genomics for US$117.6M
MGI Tech spun out as sequencing-instrument subsidiary
BGI Genomics IPO on Shenzhen ChiNext (300676), ~US$81M raised
MGI Tech IPO on Shanghai STAR Market (688114), ~US$510M raised
Named in BIOSECURE Act; bill passes US House 306–81 but stalls in Senate
BIOSECURE Act enacted as Section 851 of FY2026 NDAA

name: WuXi AppTec
nameZh: 无锡药明康德新药开发股份有限公司
shortDescription: Backbone of China's pharma services outsourcing — the end-to-end CRDMO that quietly underwrites a large share of global early-stage drug discovery.
narrativeHook: If China's innovator generation has a foundry, it is WuXi AppTec — a Shanghai-built outsourcing engine so embedded in U.S. drug pipelines that Congress wrote a law specifically to pry it loose.

timeline.status: Founded as WuXi PharmaTech in Shanghai's Waigaoqiao Free Trade Zone
timeline.note: Ge Li and Ning Zhao launch a contract-research foundry serving Western pharma — the template for China's outsourcing buildout.

timeline.status: NYSE: WX (2007–2015) → STAR + HKEX dual-listed
timeline.note: After a 2015 take-private from NYSE, WuXi spun off WuXi Biologics (HK 2017), re-listed on Shanghai's A-share market in May 2018 and on HKEX in December 2018.

timeline.status: Global CRDMO scale; BIOSECURE Act lobbying fight begins
timeline.note: FY2024 revenue RMB 39.24B; backlog RMB 49.3B (+47% YoY). U.S. House passes BIOSECURE Act naming WuXi AppTec; company publicly rejects PLA/CCP allegations and notes it has no human-genomics business.

timeline.status: Divests U.S./U.K. cell-and-gene-therapy unit; record FY2025
timeline.note: March 2025 closes sale of WuXi ATU (U.S. + U.K., incl. Oxford Genetics) to Altaris. FY2025 revenue RMB 45.46B (+15.8%); continuing-ops revenue RMB 43.42B (+21.4%); adjusted non-IFRS net profit RMB 14.96B. BIOSECURE Act signed into law via FY2026 NDAA on Dec 18 2025 — WuXi not named, but pending 1260H referral.

milestones.label: Founded by Ge Li in Shanghai's Waigaoqiao FTZ as WuXi PharmaTech
IPO on NYSE under ticker WX
Take-private from NYSE at ~$3.3B
WuXi Biologics spun off and listed in Hong Kong
Shanghai STAR/A-share IPO (603259) raises >$900M; HKEX H-share listing (02359) at HK$68
U.S. House passes BIOSECURE Act explicitly targeting WuXi AppTec
Sells WuXi ATU U.S./U.K. cell-and-gene-therapy operations to Altaris
Record FY2025 revenue of RMB 45.46B; BIOSECURE Act enacted via FY2026 NDAA

name: Sinovac Biotech
shortDescription: Vaccines, scaled massively during COVID
narrativeHook: CoronaVac became the workhorse of the developing world's COVID response — more than 2.9 billion doses shipped to over 60 countries — but the cash it generated has been frozen for years inside a U.S.-listed shell whose shares haven't traded since 2019, the longest-running governance dispute in Chinese biotech.

listings: NASDAQ: SVA (trading halted since Feb 2019; delisting determination Nov 2025, under appeal)

leadAsset.name: CoronaVac
leadAsset.indication: COVID-19 prevention
leadAsset.status: WHO EUL granted Jun 1, 2021; >2.9B doses shipped to 60+ countries by 2023; production discontinued Jan 2024

timeline.status: Founded in Beijing 2001 by Yin Weidong and Pan Aihua; Healive (China's first proprietary inactivated hepatitis A vaccine) approved 1999/launched commercially after founding
timeline.note: Listed on AMEX in 2004; uplisted to NASDAQ Global Market in 2009 as SVA

timeline.status: First H1N1 pandemic-flu vaccine (Panflu.1) approved in China 2009 — first globally to win a regulatory nod for the 2009 strain

EV71 vaccine (Inlive) for hand-foot-mouth disease commercialised in China 2016; failed go-private bid by Yin/SAIF consortium at $7/share opens decade-long boardroom war with Vivo Capital / Advantech / Prime Success
timeline.note: July 2018 Vivo-controlled board issues 11.8M dilutive PIPE shares; triggers shareholder-rights poison pill

timeline.status: Trading on NASDAQ halted Feb 22, 2019 over disputed PIPE issuance; CoronaVac Phase 3 launches across Brazil, Indonesia, Turkey, Chile in 2020
timeline.note: CoronaVac China conditional approval Feb 2021

timeline.status: WHO EUL for CoronaVac granted Jun 1, 2021 — second Chinese vaccine to win it; 2021 revenue exceeds $19B as global doses scale to ~2.7B; varicella vaccine WHO-prequalified Nov 2022
Post-COVID demand collapse — H1 2023 revenue $140M vs $1.2B prior-year H1; CoronaVac production discontinued Jan 2024; pivot back to routine vaccines (HepA Healive, sIPV, varicella, EV71, influenza)

Feb 2025 Privy Council judgment installs new board (Chiang Li chair); $55/share special cash dividend declared April 2025, paid July 7 2025 after multiple Vivo/Advantech legal challenges defeated; Nasdaq delisting determination Nov 12 2025, under appeal
timeline.note: Privatization never completed; corporate war between Yin/SAIF camp and Vivo/Advantech/Prime Success continues into 2026

milestones.label: Founded in Beijing by Yin Weidong and Pan Aihua
Listed on American Stock Exchange
Uplisted to NASDAQ (SVA); Panflu.1 H1N1 vaccine approved
Yin/SAIF consortium proposes $6.18/share go-private; Inlive (EV71) launched
Vivo-controlled board issues dilutive PIPE shares; poison pill triggered
NASDAQ halts SVA trading on Feb 22 over disputed share issuance
WHO EUL for CoronaVac (Jun 1); global rollout to 60+ countries
CoronaVac production discontinued
Privy Council installs new board; $55/share special dividend paid; Nasdaq delisting determination issued

name: RemeGen
shortDescription: Yantai-based dual A+H biotech that pioneered China's first homegrown ADC and a B-cell autoimmune fusion protein, anchoring two record license-outs to Seagen/Pfizer and Vor Bio
narrativeHook: Disitamab vedotin was the first China-discovered ADC to be out-licensed to a Western developer in a megadeal (Seagen, $2.6B in 2021), and telitacicept's 2025 NEJM Phase 3 lupus result — paired with a $4.1B Vor Bio license — turned RemeGen into a two-platform proof point that Chinese biology can sit at the global frontier in both oncology and autoimmunity.

leadAsset.name: Disitamab vedotin (Aidixi / RC48)
leadAsset.indication: HER2-expressing urothelial and breast cancers
leadAsset.status: Approved in China across four indications (incl. HER2-low breast cancer w/ liver mets, Mar 2026); Phase 3 RC48-C016 first-line urothelial met both PFS and OS endpoints (May 2025); FDA Breakthrough Therapy designation for 2L urothelial since 2020

timeline.status: Founded 2008 in Yantai by Rongchang Pharma chairman Wang Weidong and returnee scientist Jianmin Fang (Dalhousie/Harvard Medical postdoc) to build a domestic ADC and fusion-protein platform

FDA grants Breakthrough Therapy designation to disitamab vedotin (Sep 2020) for 2L HER2-positive urothelial cancer; HKEX 18A IPO Nov 9, 2020 (09995.HK), raising ~US$590M with Fidelity, BlackRock, OrbiMed, Hillhouse and Lilly Asia Ventures as cornerstones

Telitacicept (RC18) wins NMPA conditional approval for SLE (Mar 2021) — first China-discovered BlyS/APRIL dual-target fusion protein; disitamab vedotin (Aidixi) wins NMPA approval for HER2+ gastric cancer (Jun 2021) — first homegrown Chinese ADC; Seagen exclusive worldwide license-out announced Aug 9, 2021 ($200M upfront, up to $2.4B milestones, total $2.6B headline) — then China's largest-ever oncology out-license; STAR Market A-share IPO Mar 31, 2022 (688331.SH), raising ~RMB2.6B (US$412M)
timeline.note: Disitamab vedotin gains second NMPA indication (urothelial, Dec 2021)

timeline.status: Pfizer closes Seagen acquisition (Dec 2023), assuming the disitamab vedotin partnership; telitacicept wins NMPA approval for rheumatoid arthritis (Nov 2023); Phase 3 readouts advance in generalized myasthenia gravis and IgA nephropathy

Telitacicept wins NMPA approval for generalized myasthenia gravis (early 2025) and the China lupus Phase 3 is published in NEJM (Oct 2025); Vor Bio ex-China license-out announced Jun 25, 2025 — $125M initial ($45M cash + $80M warrants) plus up to $4.105B milestones; disitamab vedotin first-line urothelial Phase 3 hits PFS+OS (May 2025) and wins a fourth NMPA indication in HER2-low breast cancer (Mar 2026); FY2025 revenue RMB3.25B (+89% YoY) with first net profit of RMB708.5M
timeline.note: Global Phase 3 of telitacicept enrolling in US/EU/South America with readout expected H1 2027; FDA approval of disitamab vedotin still pending

milestones.label: RemeGen founded in Yantai by Wang Weidong and Jianmin Fang
FDA Breakthrough Therapy for disitamab vedotin; HKEX 18A IPO (09995)
Telitacicept NMPA approval for SLE; disitamab vedotin NMPA approval for HER2+ gastric; Seagen $2.6B global license-out
STAR Market A-share IPO (688331), ~RMB2.6B raised
Vor Bio ex-China license-out for telitacicept (up to $4.23B); NEJM lupus Phase 3 publication; first profitable year (RMB708.5M)
Disitamab vedotin wins fourth NMPA indication (HER2-low breast cancer with liver mets)

name: CanSino Biologics
founders: Xuefeng Yu, Tao Zhu, Dongxu Qiu, Helen Huihua Mao
shortDescription: Tianjin-based vaccine innovator built by Sanofi Pasteur returnees; first to commercialize a Chinese-developed Ebola vaccine, the world's first inhaled COVID-19 vaccine, and China's first domestic MCV4 meningococcal conjugate vaccine
narrativeHook: CanSino is the canonical 'haigui' vaccine play — four Sanofi Pasteur veterans returning to Tianjin in 2009, the first 'A+H' dual-listed vaccine company, and the platform that delivered the world's first inhaled COVID-19 vaccine before the post-pandemic revenue cliff forced it to rebuild around routine immunization.

leadAsset.name: Menhycia (MCV4)
leadAsset.mechanism: Quadrivalent meningococcal conjugate vaccine (serogroups A/C/W135/Y conjugated to CRM197)
leadAsset.indication: Meningococcal disease prevention in children 3 months to 3 years
leadAsset.status: First MCV4 approved in China (Dec 2021); Menhycia + Menphecia generated nearly RMB800M in 2024 sales (>40% YoY)

timeline.status: Founded 2009 in Tianjin by four returnees from Sanofi Pasteur, AstraZeneca, Wyeth and CNBG (Yu Xuefeng, Zhu Tao, Qiu Dongxu, Helen Mao Huihua)
timeline.note: Built around adenovirus-vector and conjugate-vaccine platforms; early backing from Qiming Venture Partners

timeline.status: HKEX 18A IPO Mar 28, 2019 (06185) — first day pop of 59%, the largest first-day gain in Hong Kong since 2017
timeline.note: Ad5-EBOV (Ebola) approved by CFDA Oct 19, 2017 for emergency use and national stockpiling — China's first domestic Ebola vaccine, second country worldwide to license one

timeline.status: Ad5-nCoV (Convidecia) authorized by NMPA Feb 25, 2021 as a single-dose COVID-19 vaccine; STAR Market dual-listing Aug 13, 2020 (688185), raising ~RMB5.2B (US$743M) — first 'A+H' vaccine company
timeline.note: Single-dose efficacy 57.5% vs symptomatic, 91.7% vs severe disease

timeline.status: Convidecia Air (inhalable Ad5-nCoV) approved Sep 4, 2022 as a booster — world's first inhaled COVID-19 vaccine; Menhycia (MCV4) approved by NMPA Dec 2021 — first quadrivalent meningococcal conjugate vaccine in China
timeline.note: WHO EUL granted to Convidecia May 19, 2022; AstraZeneca mRNA manufacturing partnership signed 2022; CanSinoBIO Switzerland subsidiary opened for European/supranational sales

timeline.status: Post-COVID revenue cliff: FY2023 revenue RMB357M (down 65% YoY) with net loss RMB1.45B; pivoting to MCV4 + pipeline
timeline.note: Menhycia exports begin to Indonesia, Middle East, South America; Halal certification granted

timeline.status: FY2024 revenue RMB846M (+137% YoY); narrowed net loss to ~RMB335–385M; CEO Yu Xuefeng targets 2025 breakeven
timeline.note: PCV13i pneumococcal vaccine pre-market inspection completed (approval expected 2025); DTcP-Hib-MCV4 combination cleared for clinical trials Feb 2025; mRNA, VLP, and adenoviral-vector platforms across 10+ disease targets

milestones.label: Founded in Tianjin by four Sanofi Pasteur / AstraZeneca / Wyeth / CNBG returnees
Ad5-EBOV Ebola vaccine approved by CFDA — China's first domestic Ebola vaccine
HKEX 18A IPO (06185) on Mar 28 — 59% first-day pop
STAR Market dual-listing (688185) — first 'A+H' vaccine company
Convidecia (Ad5-nCoV) single-dose COVID vaccine approved by NMPA; Menhycia (MCV4) approved
Convidecia Air approved Sep 4 — world's first inhaled COVID-19 vaccine; WHO EUL
FY revenue RMB846M (+137% YoY) as Menhycia franchise scales post-COVID

name: BeiGene / BeOne Medicines
shortDescription: First China-founded oncology biotech to reach global commercial scale; rebranded BeOne Medicines and redomiciled to Switzerland in May 2025
narrativeHook: Brukinsa was the first cancer drug discovered in China to win FDA approval (Nov 2019) and the first to beat a Western standard of care head-to-head (vs Imbruvica in ALPINE) — the moment China's innovation era became unignorable to global pharma.

leadAsset.name: Brukinsa (zanubrutinib)
leadAsset.indication: B-cell malignancies (CLL, WM, MCL, MZL, FL+obinutuzumab)
leadAsset.status: Approved in 75 markets; $2.6B FY2024 revenue (105% YoY)

timeline.status: Founded in Beijing by Oyler (entrepreneur) and Wang (HHMI investigator at NIBS Beijing)
timeline.note: Early Merck $20M investment; built around running global-standard trials inside China

timeline.status: NASDAQ IPO Feb 3, 2016 at $24/ADS (BGNE)
Hong Kong dual listing Aug 8, 2018 at HK$108/share — first Chapter 18A IPO by a US-listed biotech (US$903M raised)
timeline.note: Oct 2019: Amgen takes 20.5% stake at $174.85/ADS for $2.7B; joint development of 20 oncology assets in China

timeline.status: Brukinsa wins FDA accelerated approval Nov 14, 2019 for mantle cell lymphoma — first cancer drug discovered in China to clear FDA
STAR Market triple listing Dec 15, 2021 (688235), RMB22.2B (US$3.5B) raised — first triple-listed biotech globally
timeline.note: Tislelizumab (later Tevimbra) approved by NMPA; multiple CLL/WM label expansions for Brukinsa

timeline.status: ALPINE trial: Brukinsa beats ibrutinib head-to-head in R/R CLL — first China-discovered drug to defeat a Western standard of care
timeline.note: Recoups Tevimbra rights from Novartis

timeline.status: Renamed BeOne Medicines and redomiciled Cayman → Switzerland (effective May 27, 2025); ticker NASDAQ:BGNE → ONC; first GAAP-profitable quarter Q1 2025; FY2025 revenue guide $4.9–5.3B
timeline.note: Brukinsa now in 75 markets (US$2.6B FY2024); Tevimbra in 46 markets with new FDA esophageal SCC indication

name: Innovent Biologics
shortDescription: Suzhou-based PD-1 pioneer that built the template for China-MNC co-development through Eli Lilly, then pivoted to global out-licensing with the Takeda mega-deal and NMPA approval of the world's first GCG/GLP-1 dual agonist mazdutide
narrativeHook: Innovent's Tyvyt was the only PD-1 admitted to China's NRDL in 2019 (at a 64% price cut) and the FDA's Feb 2022 rejection of its Lilly-partnered BLA — on the basis that an all-China trial couldn't represent a US population — became the cautionary case study that reshaped how Chinese biotechs design global pivotals.

leadAsset.name: Tyvyt (sintilimab)
leadAsset.indication: Hodgkin lymphoma, NSCLC (squamous and nonsquamous), HCC, gastric/GEJ, esophageal SCC
leadAsset.status: Approved in China (Dec 2018); only PD-1 in NRDL since 2019; FDA CRL Feb 2022, Lilly returned ex-China rights Oct 2022

timeline.status: Founded by Michael Yu (Yu Dechao) at BioBay in Suzhou Industrial Park (Aug 24, 2011); Cayman parent incorporated April 28, 2011
timeline.note: Founder was a US-trained molecular geneticist (CAS PhD, UCSF post-doc) who returned to build a biologics platform aimed at affordable mAbs for the Chinese market

timeline.status: Strategic alliance with Eli Lilly signed March 2015 — $56M upfront plus up to ~$400M in milestones to co-develop three antibodies; expanded October 2015 to add three more oncology assets, totaling potential value north of $1B
timeline.note: First major MNC-Chinese-biotech co-development deal in oncology; became the template for the China-out licensing model that followed

timeline.status: HKEX 18A listing on October 31, 2018 (ticker 01801) raising HK$3.8B (~US$485M); sintilimab approved by NMPA on December 27, 2018 for relapsed/refractory classical Hodgkin lymphoma
timeline.note: IFR Asia-Pacific IPO of the Year 2018; stock jumped 19% on debut

timeline.status: Tyvyt admitted to China's NRDL November 2019 — first PD-1 on the list, at a 64% price cut; rapid label expansion through 2020–2021 across NSCLC, HCC, gastric and esophageal cancers
timeline.note: Lilly paid Innovent $200M upfront + up to $825M in milestones (Aug 2020) for ex-China rights to sintilimab — meant to be the first China-discovered cancer drug to reach Western markets

timeline.status: FDA Complete Response Letter for sintilimab + chemo in 1L nonsquamous NSCLC issued February 24, 2022 — ODAC voted 14–1 against, citing the all-China ORIENT-11 trial's lack of US-applicability; Lilly terminated ex-China license October 2022 and returned rights
timeline.note: Inflection point that forced China biotechs to embrace multi-regional clinical trial design; FDA later issued draft guidance on oncology MRCTs as a direct consequence

timeline.status: Roche signs exclusive global license for IBI3009 (DLL3 ADC) in December 2024 with potential value above $1B; product portfolio expands to ~16 commercialized assets across oncology, ophthalmology, autoimmune, and cardiometabolic disease
timeline.note: Pivots from PD-1 dependence to a diversified pipeline anchored in bispecifics (PD-1/IL-2, VEGF/ANG2) and ADCs (CLDN18.2, DLL3)

timeline.status: Mazdutide (GCG/GLP-1 dual agonist licensed from Lilly) wins NMPA approval June 27, 2025 for chronic weight management — world's first dual-agonist GLP-1 approved for obesity; Sept 2025 second approval for T2D glycemic control. Takeda licenses IBI363 (PD-1/IL-2α-bias bispecific) and IBI343 (CLDN18.2 ADC) for ex-Greater China rights on October 21, 2025: US$1.2B upfront (incl. $100M equity) and up to ~$11.4B total. FY2025 revenue RMB 13.0B (+38.4%), product revenue RMB 11.9B (+44.6%); first full year of GAAP profitability (RMB 814M IFRS net income). 18 commercialized products as of year-end 2025; TABOSUN (ipilimumab N01) — China's first domestic anti-CTLA-4 — approved December 25, 2025
timeline.note: New Lilly oncology/immunology pact (Feb 2026) with potential milestones up to ~$8.9B further deepens the founding partnership

milestones.label: Founded in Suzhou by Michael Yu (Yu Dechao)
Eli Lilly oncology co-development alliance — $56M upfront, template for China-MNC deals
HKEX 18A IPO (01801) raises ~US$485M; first sintilimab NMPA approval
Tyvyt added to NRDL at 64% price cut — only PD-1 on the list

shortDescription: First China-discovered PD-1 approved by NMPA
narrativeHook: Toripalimab — the first homegrown Chinese PD-1 — and the first FDA-approved Chinese-developed checkpoint inhibitor, validating that domestic R&D could clear US regulatory bars on its own clinical evidence.

leadAsset.name: Toripalimab (Tuoyi / Loqtorzi)
leadAsset.indication: Nasopharyngeal carcinoma; melanoma; NSCLC; renal cell, urothelial, esophageal, TNBC, SCLC and other solid tumors

timeline.status: Founded December 2012 in Shanghai by Xiong Jun, Li Ning, and Zhang Zhuobing as an innovation-driven biopharma; toripalimab program initiated.

name: Akeso

name: Legend Biotech
shortDescription: GenScript-spun cell therapy company that co-developed Carvykti (cilta-cel) — the world's deepest-responding BCMA CAR-T — with Janssen, becoming the first China-discovered cell therapy to reach blockbuster scale on the global market
narrativeHook: Legend's $350M-upfront deal with Janssen in December 2017 — struck on the back of an investigator-initiated trial in Xi'an showing 100% response in r/r myeloma — was the moment a Chinese-discovered cell therapy first commanded MNC big-pharma terms; Carvykti's $1.9B in 2025 net trade sales is the validation.

leadAsset.name: Carvykti (ciltacabtagene autoleucel)
leadAsset.mechanism: BCMA-directed autologous CAR-T cell therapy with dual VHH binding domains
leadAsset.indication: Relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma (2L+ since April 2024)
leadAsset.status: Approved in 14 markets across 294 treatment sites; $1.9B FY2025 net trade sales (50/50 P&L split with J&J); franchise reached profitability in 2025

timeline.status: Founded in 2014 as a subsidiary of Nanjing-based GenScript Biotech (founded 2002 in New Jersey by Frank Zhang) to commercialize cell-therapy research; initial focus on TCR and CAR-T platforms
timeline.note: Legend's lead BCMA CAR-T construct (LCAR-B38M, later cilta-cel) was designed by a research team led by Fan Xiaohu in Nanjing; uses two BCMA-binding camelid VHH domains to drive higher avidity than competing single-domain BCMA CAR-Ts

timeline.status: Investigator-initiated LEGEND-2 trial at Xi'an Jiaotong's Second Affiliated Hospital reports 100% ORR in r/r multiple myeloma at ASCO 2017 — the dataset that triggered the Janssen deal; exclusive worldwide license & collaboration agreement with Janssen Biotech (J&J) signed December 2017 with $350M upfront plus development, regulatory, and sales milestones; co-development shifts to Phase 1b/2 CARTITUDE-1 in the US
timeline.note: Largest upfront ever paid to a Chinese biotech for a single asset at the time; structured as 50/50 cost and profit share ex-Greater China, 30/70 (Legend/Janssen) inside Greater China

timeline.status: NASDAQ IPO on June 5, 2020 (ticker LEGN) at $23.00 per ADS; with full greenshoe exercised, Legend issued 21,188,750 ADSs for total gross proceeds of $487.3M, the largest US biotech IPO of 2020 to that point; concurrent $25M private placement from parent GenScript
timeline.note: Cayman-domiciled holdco; GenScript retained majority ownership post-IPO. CARTITUDE-1 primary readout (ORR 97.9%) anchored a rolling BLA submission with FDA in late 2020

timeline.status: FDA approval of Carvykti (ciltacabtagene autoleucel) on February 28, 2022 for r/r multiple myeloma after 4+ prior lines — making it the second BCMA CAR-T after Bristol/2seventy's Abecma and the first cell therapy fully co-developed with a Chinese partner; EU conditional marketing authorization granted by European Commission on May 25, 2022; Japan MHLW approval September 2022
timeline.note: Manufacturing initially throttled by viral-vector and capacity bottlenecks at the Raritan, NJ launch site; a problem that defined the franchise's first 18 months

timeline.status: FDA expanded Carvykti's label to 2L+ on April 5, 2024 based on CARTITUDE-4 (59% reduction in risk of disease progression or death vs standard of care); EC matched on April 23, 2024. Manufacturing scale-up: Ghent (Obelisc) site begins commercial production Q3 2024; J&J/Legend commit additional ~$150M to second Ghent facility; FY2024 Carvykti net trade sales $963M (+93% YoY), with $482.6M in collaboration revenue booked by Legend
timeline.note: First and only BCMA CAR-T approved as second-line therapy in MM; scale-out into community treatment sites accelerates patient access

timeline.status: FY2025 results (reported March 10, 2026): Carvykti net trade sales $1.9B (+97% YoY), Q4 alone $555M; total Legend revenue $1,028.9M; net loss $296.8M; cash and time deposits ~$949M. Franchise reaches profitability for the first time. >10,000 cumulative patients treated; product available at 294 sites across 14 markets. Raritan expansion completed — now the largest cell-therapy manufacturing site in the US, capable of supporting up to 10,000 patients/year. First in-human readout for LUCAR-G39D (allogeneic CAR-T); first dosing of in vivo CAR-T candidates; 31,000 sq-ft R&D facility opens in Philadelphia. Persistent J&J takeover speculation through 2025–26
timeline.pipelineStage: Marketed + Phase 1 (allogeneic, in vivo, solid tumor)
timeline.note: Solid-tumor CAR-Ts LB1908 (Claudin 18.2) and LB2102 (DLL3, licensed to Novartis Nov 2023) advance through dose-escalation

name: WuXi Biologics
shortDescription: Global biologics CRDMO spun out of WuXi AppTec; the world's second-largest biologics contract developer and the most-watched name on the BIOSECURE Act target list.
narrativeHook: When BIOSECURE named WuXi Biologics in 2024, every Western biotech with a Chinese manufacturing tail had to decide whether China-built CMC was a strategic asset or a political liability — and Chris Chen's answer was to break ground in Singapore.

timeline.status: Internal biologics unit built inside WuXi AppTec from 2010, formally incorporated as WuXi Biologics (Cayman) in 2014.
timeline.note: Origin: a small mAb services group within Ge Li's WuXi PharmaTech, run by Chris Chen after he joined from Celgene Biopharmaceuticals in 2011.

timeline.status: Carved out as a standalone entity in 2015 alongside WuXi PharmaTech's NYSE take-private; positioned as a pure-play biologics CRDMO.
timeline.note: Spinoff timed with the parent's restructuring after the $3.3B NYSE take-private led by Ge Li.

timeline.status: IPO on HKEX (2269) on 13 June 2017, raising ~HK$4.0B (~US$510M) at HK$20.60/share — the largest Hong Kong listing of 2017 at the time.
timeline.note: Listed before the 18A chapter formally opened, but became the template for the HK biotech IPO wave.

timeline.status: Joined the Hang Seng Index in August 2020; opened Hangzhou site (acquired from Pfizer) and the Dundalk, Ireland mega-facility under construction.
timeline.note: Pandemic-era CDMO surge: signed COVID antibody and vaccine MSAs with Eli Lilly, Vir/GSK, and others.

timeline.status: Dundalk Ireland (~€325M / 2.79M sq ft) and Worcester MA sites came online; integrated project count crossed 700.
timeline.note: Built out the 'Global Dual Sourcing' footprint — China + ex-China for the same molecule — partly to hedge regulatory risk.

timeline.status: Spun off WuXi XDC on HKEX (2268) on 17 Nov 2023, raising ~HK$4.07B (~US$520M); broke ground on a $1.4B Singapore CRDMO campus in Tuas Biomedical Park.
timeline.note: Singapore announcement came weeks after BIOSECURE's draft text was reported in early 2024 — a pre-emptive de-risking move under the 'Global Dual Sourcing' banner.

timeline.status: FY2024 revenue RMB 18.68B (+9.6% YoY); 817 integrated projects, 66 in Phase III, 21 non-COVID commercial. Sold WuXi Vaccines' Dundalk Ireland facility to MSD for ~$500M (Jan 2025) and exited the Hang Seng Index.
timeline.note: Named in 2024 House and Senate BIOSECURE drafts; dropped from the final NDAA-attached version that became law in Dec 2025, but reputational drag persists.

name: CStone Pharmaceuticals
shortDescription: Suzhou-based precision oncology innovator known for sugemalimab (Cejemly) and an in-licensed precision-oncology portfolio commercialised in China
narrativeHook: CStone built its early business by stitching together world-class licensing partnerships — Blueprint, Pfizer, Agios, Pfizer again — and using them to learn how to run global trials. Sugemalimab's 2024 EMA approval made it the first Chinese-discovered anti-PD-L1 antibody to clear a Western regulator, even as the EQRx implosion and FDA's China-only data stance closed the U.S. door.

name: Abogen Biosciences
shortDescription: China's flagship mRNA platform; co-developer of ARCoV/AWcorna, China's first homegrown mRNA COVID-19 vaccine.
narrativeHook: Founded in January 2019 by ex-Moderna scientist Bo Ying, Abogen went from a Suzhou startup to a unicorn in two years and set a Chinese biotech fundraising record with a $700M Series C in August 2021 — proof that China could field a credible mRNA platform of its own.

leadAsset.name: ARCoV / AWcorna
leadAsset.indication: COVID-19
leadAsset.status: EUA in Indonesia (Sep 2022); not approved in mainland China

timeline.status: Founded January 2019 in Suzhou by Bo Ying (ex-Moderna, ex-Dicerna). ARCoV mRNA COVID-19 vaccine — co-developed with Walvax Biotechnology and the PLA Academy of Military Sciences (AMMS) — became the first mRNA vaccine approved for clinical trials in China.

Raised >$700M Series C in August 2021 (Temasek, Invesco, LVC, GL Ventures, Yunfeng, Lilly Asia Ventures, Boyu) — the largest equity round ever for a China-based biopharma at the time — followed by a $300M Series C+ in November 2021 led by SoftBank Vision Fund and 5Y Capital. Phase 3 trials for ARCoV launched in Mexico, Indonesia, and Nepal. AWcorna received Indonesia BPOM emergency use authorization on 30 September 2022 — the first China-made mRNA vaccine authorized anywhere — but never won NMPA approval at home.
timeline.note: Cumulative funding >$1.13B across five rounds.

timeline.status: Pivoted from COVID-only to a diversified mRNA pipeline spanning oncology, infectious disease, and rare disease. Lead oncology asset ABO2011 (IL-12 mRNA) entered a Phase 1/2 trial as monotherapy or in combination with toripalimab in advanced solid tumors (NCT06088004); recruitment expanded through 2023–2024.

Received FDA IND clearance (May 2025) and NMPA CDE IND clearance (August 2025) for ABO2102, a KRAS multi-mutation therapeutic cancer vaccine — described as China's first therapeutic cancer vaccine targeting five common KRAS mutations. NMPA also cleared INDs for lyophilized mRNA shingles and RSV vaccine candidates in early 2025.

name: Sinopharm / CNBG
nameZh: 国药集团 / 中国生物
shortDescription: Central state-owned pharmaceutical group whose CNBG vaccine arm produced BBIBP-CorV, the first non-Western COVID-19 vaccine on the WHO Emergency Use Listing.
narrativeHook: When WHO greenlit BBIBP-CorV in May 2021, a Beijing state vaccine plant became the supply backbone of COVAX outside the West — proof that China's century-old biologics infrastructure could move at pandemic speed.

timeline.status: Sinopharm Group Co. Ltd. (51% CNPGC / 49% Fosun) listed H-shares on HKEX as 1099 in 2009; absorbed CNBG the same year.
CNBG's Beijing Institute (BIBP) and Wuhan Institute (WIBP) developed inactivated COVID-19 vaccines BBIBP-CorV and WIBP-CorV; both received China conditional approval in late 2020.

timeline.status: FY2024 revenue RMB 584.5B (~USD 80B); ranked 119 on 2025 Fortune Global 500. Shanghai Institute (SIBP) launched first mRNA vaccine clinical trial (herpes zoster) in April 2025.

timeline.status: Built out conjugate-vaccine portfolio — meningococcal A/C and ACYW135 conjugates; exports to Indonesia and Egypt; Gates Foundation partnership (2015) to support HPV9 development.

23-valent pneumococcal vaccine first export to Latin America (El Salvador) and bivalent HPV approved in Indonesia in 2025; mRNA pipeline expanded with NMPA IND clearances for herpes zoster mRNA (Dec 2025) and RSV mRNA (Oct 2025, with Fudan University and Shanghai Lanchen).

name: Shanghai Henlius Biotech
nameZh: 上海复宏汉霖生物技术
founders: Scott Liu (Liu Shikao), Wenjie Zhang; co-founded with Fosun Pharma
shortDescription: Fosun Pharma-backed biologics company that delivered China's first homegrown monoclonal-antibody biosimilar (Hanlikang/rituximab, 2019) and the first China-developed biosimilar approved in the EU and the US (Hanquyou/Hercessi, trastuzumab), and is now globalizing its in-house anti-PD-1 serplulimab
narrativeHook: Henlius proved that a Chinese biotech could write the biosimilar playbook for itself: HLX01 was the first mAb biosimilar approved under NMPA's 2015 guideline, and HLX02 (Zercepac/Hercessi) became the first Chinese-developed monoclonal antibody to clear both EMA and FDA — a quiet but decisive answer to the question of whether China could ship cGMP biologics into Western markets.

leadAsset.name: Hansizhuang (serplulimab)

timeline.status: Founded February 2010 in Shanghai by Dr. Scott Liu (a former Amgen director who returned to China after his father's liver-cancer death) together with Fosun Pharma; mission framed as making expensive biologics affordable for Chinese patients
timeline.note: Liu took the 'Han' in 'Henlius' (汉霖) from his father's given name; Fosun Pharma anchored the venture with capital and infrastructure from day one

timeline.status: Pre-IPO build-out: head-to-head Phase 3 of HLX02 (trastuzumab) launched globally; commercial Xuhui facility (24,000L) achieved China and EU GMP; HLX01 NDA filed 2017
timeline.note: Positioned to be the first mover under NMPA's Feb 2015 Technical Guidelines for Biosimilars

timeline.status: HLX01 (Hanlikang, rituximab biosimilar) approved by NMPA on Feb 22, 2019 — China's first NMPA-approved homegrown monoclonal-antibody biosimilar; HKEX 18A IPO completed Sep 25, 2019 (ticker 02696) at HK$49.60/share, raising ~US$410M, the largest HK biotech IPO of the year
timeline.note: Joint sponsors CICC, Bank of America, CMB International, Fosun Hani Securities and Citi; ~40% of proceeds earmarked for Core Product clinical development

timeline.status: HLX02 (Hanquyou / Zercepac, trastuzumab biosimilar) received European Commission approval Jul 27, 2020 — first China-developed monoclonal antibody approved in the EU — followed by NMPA approval in August 2020
timeline.note: Commercialized in Europe with partner Accord Healthcare; broke open the western biosimilar market for Chinese mAb manufacturers

timeline.status: HANSIZHUANG (serplulimab), Henlius' first in-house innovative mAb, approved by NMPA Mar 2022 for MSI-H solid tumors — its first tissue-agnostic PD-1 indication in China; Organon global license for HLX11 (pertuzumab) and HLX14 (denosumab) biosimilars signed June 2022 — up to US$541M including US$73M upfront
timeline.note: Pivot from biosimilar specialist to integrated biopharma underway; Hanbeitai (bevacizumab) and Handayuan (adalimumab) added to commercial portfolio

timeline.status: Serplulimab approved by NMPA for ES-SCLC (Mar 2023) — world's first anti-PD-1 mAb approved for first-line ES-SCLC, based on the international ASTRUM-005 trial; HLX02 (HERCESSI / trastuzumab-strf) FDA-approved Apr 26, 2024 with Accord BioPharma — first Henlius product on the US market and first Chinese-developed biosimilar to clear both EMA and FDA; Fosun Pharma announced a HK$5.407B (US$692M / HK$24.60 per H share) take-private offer for the ~40.4% it did not own on June 24, 2024
timeline.note: FY2024 revenue RMB 5.7244B (+6.1% YoY); net profit RMB 820.5M (+50.3% YoY) — second consecutive profitable year

timeline.status: Privatization rejected at H-share class meeting Jan 22, 2025 (opposition >10% threshold) — Henlius remains HKEX-listed, Fosun Pharma stays controlling shareholder at ~59.6%. Hetronifly (serplulimab) granted EU marketing authorisation Feb 4, 2025 — first anti-PD-1 approved in the EU for ES-SCLC, launched in 12 EU countries within a year. HLX11 (pertuzumab biosimilar) FDA-approved as POHERDY in 2H 2025 — first US-approved pertuzumab biosimilar; HLX14 (denosumab) approved in US/EU/UK/Canada (BILDYOS / BILPREVDA). Pipeline includes ~20 clinical and ~40 preclinical molecules across PD-L1 ADC (HLX43), HER2 mAb (HLX22), tri-/tetra-specific T-cell engagers and a CDK4/6 inhibitor (FUTUONING). FY2025 revenue RMB 6.6666B (+16.5% YoY), net profit RMB 827M; R&D RMB 2.49B (+35.4%)
timeline.note: Eisai signed exclusive Japan license for serplulimab in 2026; HK$43.5B market cap as of late April 2026 (~US$5.6B) reflects re-rating around innovative pipeline

name: Tigermed
shortDescription: China's largest clinical CRO; multinational trial support
narrativeHook: Tigermed is the Hangzhou-built clinical-trial backbone of China's innovator generation — the only Chinese CRO inside the global top ten, running BeiGene's Brukinsa registrational program and the in-country trials of nearly every multinational sponsor.

timeline.status: Founded in Hangzhou by Ye Xiaoping and Cao Xiaochun
timeline.note: Ye, a former Shanghai Roche medical director with an Oxford PhD, teams with ex-Jiuyuan Gene executive Cao Xiaochun to launch a domestic clinical CRO at the moment China is harmonising with ICH-style trial standards.

timeline.status: ChiNext IPO (SZSE: 300347)
timeline.note: August 17 2012 listing on Shenzhen ChiNext at RMB 37.88, raising ~RMB 508M. Becomes the public-market vehicle through which Chinese biotechs (BeiGene, Innovent, Junshi, Akeso) buy clinical-trial capacity.

timeline.status: Spins off U.S. preclinical arm Frontage Holdings (HKEX: 1521)
timeline.note: May 30 2019 — Pennsylvania-based Frontage lists on HKEX; Tigermed retains majority control. Cements a U.S./China bioanalytical and DMPK footprint as Chinese sponsors begin filing FDA INDs in volume.

timeline.status: Hong Kong dual listing (HKEX: 03347)
timeline.note: August 7 2020 — prices at HK$100, raises ~US$1.38B in Asia's largest healthcare listing of the year. Davis Polk advises on the HK$10.7B offering.

timeline.status: China clinical-CRO market leader; FY2024 revenue RMB 6.60B (-10.6%)
timeline.note: Holds 12.8% of China's clinical CRO market in 2023 — the only Chinese CRO in the global top ten (1.4% global share). FY2024 revenue RMB 6,603M (-10.6%); adjusted net profit RMB 855M (-42.1%); net new bookings RMB 8.42B (+7.3%); backlog RMB 15.78B. Supports 28 Class I new drug + 6 innovative device approvals in 2024.

timeline.status: FY2025 recovery; Japan and Shanghai bolt-on M&A
timeline.note: FY2025 revenue RMB 6.83B (+3.5%); net profit attributable to parent RMB 888M (+119%). Net new contract value RMB 10.16B (+20.7%); backlog RMB 18.20B (+15.3%). Acquires Japanese CRO Micron and Shanghai Bioquick; opens new Hangzhou global HQ; awarded MSCI ESG AAA. BIOSECURE Act enacted via FY2026 NDAA (Dec 18 2025) does not name Tigermed, but the law reshapes the China-CRO competitive landscape.

milestones.label: Founded in Hangzhou by Ye Xiaoping and Cao Xiaochun
ChiNext IPO on Shenzhen Stock Exchange (300347), raising ~RMB 508M
U.S. preclinical subsidiary Frontage Holdings IPOs on HKEX (1521.HK)
HKEX secondary listing (03347) prices at HK$100, raising ~US$1.38B
FY2024 revenue RMB 6.60B; 12.8% China clinical-CRO market share
FY2025 revenue RMB 6.83B; acquires Japanese CRO Micron and Shanghai Bioquick

name: Pharmaron
shortDescription: Full-service CRO/CDMO spanning discovery chemistry, biology, CMC and commercial manufacturing across China, the UK and the US
narrativeHook: The other Chinese R&D engine after WuXi — Boliang Lou built Pharmaron from a 20-person Beijing chemistry shop in 2004 into a globally distributed, dual-listed CRO/CDMO with major UK biologics and gene-therapy capacity in Hoddesdon and Liverpool, positioning it as the most-watched alternative if BIOSECURE expands beyond the WuXi names.

leadAsset.name: Integrated CRO/CDMO platform
leadAsset.indication: All therapeutic areas (small molecule + biologics + cell & gene therapy)
leadAsset.status: FY2024 revenue RMB 12,275.8M (~US$1.70B), +6.4% YoY; H1 2025 revenue RMB 6,441.0M, +14.9% YoY

timeline.status: Founded 2004 in the Beijing Economic-Technological Development Area by Boliang Lou after his return from the US; opened first chemistry lab with ~20 staff
Acquired MSD's Hoddesdon UK process R&D site (2017), establishing the UK chemistry footprint; listed on Shenzhen ChiNext as 300759 in Jan 2018
Listed H-shares on HKEX as 03759 on 28 Nov 2019, becoming a dual-listed CRO/CDMO; expanded UK and US footprints via tuck-in acquisitions
Acquired AbbVie's Liverpool biologics plant (2021) — anchor of Pharmaron Biologics gene-therapy CDMO; bought Absorption Systems (US) for US$137.5M, adding San Diego/Boston/Philadelphia labs
timeline.pipelineStage: Building biologics + cell & gene therapy CDMO arm

timeline.status: Announced £151M expansion of Liverpool gene-therapy CDMO facility; capacity expected to roughly quadruple — positioned as a non-WuXi global CDMO option as BIOSECURE debate intensifies
FY2024 revenue RMB 12,275.8M (+6.4%), profit RMB 1,793.4M (+12%); H1 2025 revenue +14.9%; Pharmaron not named in the Dec 2025 BIOSECURE Act but watchlisted by analysts as a potential future addition

milestones.label: Founded in Beijing by Boliang Lou; first chemistry lab opens with ~20 staff
Acquires MSD's Hoddesdon UK process R&D site — first major UK foothold
Lists on Shenzhen ChiNext (300759) in January
Lists H-shares on HKEX (03759) on 28 November
Acquires AbbVie's Liverpool biologics plant; closes Absorption Systems acquisition (US$137.5M) for US labs in San Diego, Boston and Philadelphia
Announces £151M expansion of Liverpool gene-therapy CDMO facility
FY2024 revenue RMB 12.28B (+6.4%); BIOSECURE Act spotlight elevates demand for non-WuXi CRO/CDMO capacity
H1 2025 revenue RMB 6.44B, +14.9%; Pharmaron omitted from final BIOSECURE Act enacted Dec 2025

name: Asymchem
shortDescription: Tianjin-based small-molecule API/CDMO; pioneer of continuous-flow chemistry and a leading GLP-1 peptide manufacturer; A+H dual-listed (SZSE 002821 / HKEX 6821).
signature: A+H dual-listed CDMO; Pfizer Paxlovid supplier
narrativeHook: Founded by US-returnee chemist Hao Hong, Asymchem rode the Paxlovid surge of 2022 to become one of China's most globally embedded small-molecule CDMOs — and one of the few to take over a Pfizer plant on Western soil.

timeline.status: Founded in TEDA Tianjin by Hao Hong after returning from his US-incorporated Asymchem (1995, Research Triangle Park, NC); positioned as a small-molecule custom-synthesis CMO for Western pharma.
timeline.note: Tianjin entity established 1998; corporate documents also cite 1999 as the formal founding year.

timeline.status: Listed A-shares on Shenzhen (SME Board) on 18 November 2016 under code 002821 at RMB 30.53/share — among the first wave of CRO/CDMO IPOs after Bi Jingquan's 722 reform.
Scaled continuous-flow / green-chemistry capacity, becoming a strategic supplier to Pfizer, Merck, and Eli Lilly for late-stage and commercial small-molecule APIs.

Closed an A+H dual listing on 10 December 2021 (HKEX: 6821) raising HK$7.15B — the largest healthcare IPO in Hong Kong that year. In Feb 2022, subsidiary Jilin Asymchem signed Paxlovid-related supply contracts worth ~RMB 3.54B with Pfizer; Jilin Asymchem is named on Paxlovid's FDA DailyMed label as a manufacturer.
timeline.note: FY2022 revenue surged on Paxlovid demand to RMB 10.3B before normalising.

timeline.status: FY2024 revenue declined to RMB 5.80B (-25% YoY) as the COVID-era Paxlovid contract wound down; net profit RMB 949M. Pivoted aggressively to GLP-1 peptide CDMO and oligonucleotide capacity.
timeline.note: August 2024: inaugurated first European site at Discovery Park, Sandwich, Kent — taking over a former Pfizer small-molecule API pilot plant and R&D labs.

timeline.status: Operating as a globally distributed CDMO across Tianjin, Jilin, Liaoning, the US, and the UK; flagged in BIOSECURE Act discourse but not named in the bill, unlike WuXi peers.

milestones.label: Asymchem Inc. founded in Research Triangle Park, NC by Hao Hong
Tianjin operations established in TEDA
SZSE A-share IPO (002821) on 18 November
HKEX H-share IPO (6821) on 10 December — A+H dual listing complete; HK$7.15B raised
Jilin Asymchem signs ~RMB 3.54B Paxlovid supply contracts with Pfizer
Inaugurates Sandwich, UK site (former Pfizer API plant); FY2024 revenue RMB 5.80B

name: Mabwell
nameZh: 迈威（上海）生物科技股份有限公司
shortDescription: Shanghai-based biologics company that rolled up nine antibody and recombinant-protein assets into a STAR-listed full-stack biopharma, then leveraged its IDDC ADC platform into back-to-back deals with Disc Medicine and Alphabet's Calico.
narrativeHook: Mabwell built itself by acquisition rather than founder-startup, then converted a biosimilar cash engine (denosumab, adalimumab) into the runway for an ADC platform (IDDC) that landed a $412M Disc Medicine deal in 2023, a $596M Calico IL-11 deal in 2025, and the world's first Nectin-4 ADC Phase III in TNBC by 2026.

leadAsset.name: 9MW2821 (bulumtatug fuvedotin)
leadAsset.indication: Urothelial carcinoma, cervical cancer, triple-negative breast cancer
leadAsset.status: World's first Nectin-4 ADC in Phase III; three pivotal trials running (UC, CC, TNBC); FDA Fast Track for TNBC; first US patient dosed Aug 2025; NEJM case report on hepatic pseudoprogression in cervical cancer (Apr 2026)

timeline.status: Founded 2017 in Shanghai's Zhangjiang High-Tech Park by Dr. Datao Liu; assembled by absorbing nine biopharmaceutical R&D and manufacturing entities (incl. Jiangsu T-mab in Taizhou) rather than starting from a single lab
timeline.note: Co-founder/CSO Hai Wu was a founding scientist of Junshi Biosciences before joining; the roll-up gave Mabwell mAb, recombinant-protein, and process-development capacity from day one

timeline.status: Built out manufacturing footprint (Taizhou pilot + commercial site, Shanghai large-scale base, dedicated ADC commercial site); IDDC™ (Interchain-Disulfide Drug Conjugate) ADC platform established 2018; first IND filings for biosimilar and novel mAb pipeline
timeline.note: Co-development of adalimumab biosimilar 9MW0113 with Junshi Biosciences

timeline.status: STAR Market IPO on Jan 18, 2022 (688062.SH) raised RMB 3.48B (~US$548M) — though oversubscribed, shares fell ~30% on debut to RMB 24.50; JUNMAIKANG (9MW0113, adalimumab biosimilar, co-developed with Junshi) NMPA-approved Mar 2022
timeline.note: Cool IPO reception foreshadowed the broader STAR biotech valuation reset; proceeds earmarked for ADC platform build-out

timeline.status: Disc Medicine $412.5M ex-Greater-China license signed Jan 2023 for 9MW3011 (anti-TMPRSS6 mAb / MWTX-003) for β-thalassemia and polycythemia vera (FDA Fast Track later granted); MAILISHU (9MW0321, denosumab biosimilar 60mg) NMPA-approved Mar 31, 2023 — world's second denosumab biosimilar; MAIWEIJIAN (9MW0311, denosumab 120mg) NMPA-approved Mar 2024 — first denosumab 120mg biosimilar in China; FY2024 revenue RMB 199.78M (+56.3% YoY) on net loss ~RMB 1.04B
timeline.note: Disc Medicine deal validated the discovery output of Mabwell Therapeutics (San Diego subsidiary); biosimilar cash flow underwriting the ADC platform

timeline.status: MAILISHENG (long-acting albumin-fusion G-CSF) launched 2025 — fourth approved product; Calico Life Sciences (Alphabet) signed $596M ex-Greater-China license for 9MW3811 (anti-IL-11 mAb) on Jun 26, 2025 ($25M upfront + up to $571M milestones); 9MW2821 (Nectin-4 ADC) advanced to three Phase III pivotal trials (UC, CC, TNBC) with FDA Fast Track for TNBC; HKEX secondary listing on Apr 28, 2026 (02563) raised HK$1.45B (~US$185M) at HK$27.64/share to fund ADC global development; market cap ~US$2.2B at HK listing
timeline.note: Calico tie-up extends the licensing pattern into longevity/anti-aging biology; the Hong Kong primary went to international institutions at 90% allocation, signaling foreign appetite for Chinese ADC platforms despite BIOSECURE noise

milestones.label: Founded in Shanghai by Datao Liu via roll-up of nine biopharma entities
IDDC™ ADC platform established
STAR Market IPO (688062) — RMB 3.48B raised; JUNMAIKANG (adalimumab) NMPA approval
Disc Medicine license-out for 9MW3011 (~$412.5M); MAILISHU (denosumab 60mg) NMPA approval
MAIWEIJIAN (denosumab 120mg) NMPA approval — first 120mg denosumab biosimilar in China
Calico Life Sciences IL-11 license-out (~$596M); MAILISHENG (long-acting G-CSF) launch
HKEX secondary listing (02563) raises ~US$185M; 9MW2821 enters world-first Nectin-4 ADC Phase III in TNBC

name: Fosun Kite / Fosun Kairos
nameZh: 复星凯特生物科技 / 复星凯瑞斯
founders: Shanghai Fosun Pharmaceutical (Group) and Kite Pharma (Gilead)
shortDescription: Shanghai-based cell-therapy company that won China's first CAR-T approval (axicabtagene ciloleucel, June 2021) as a 50/50 Fosun Pharma–Gilead/Kite JV; bought out by Fosun in September 2024 and rebranded Fosun Kairos.
narrativeHook: Fosun Kite localized Yescarta into China and shipped the country's first commercial CAR-T in June 2021, but the deal did what JVs in single-payer markets often do — Beijing's reimbursement squeeze made the economics unworkable for Gilead, which exited eight years in for $27M, leaving Fosun with full control of the platform and a renamed Fosun Kairos.

leadAsset.name: Yikaida (FKC876, axicabtagene ciloleucel)
leadAsset.indication: Relapsed/refractory large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL, PMBCL, HGBCL, transformed FL); second-line LBCL indication filed
leadAsset.status: China's first approved CAR-T (NMPA, 23 June 2021); included in Shanghai 'Hu Hui Bao' inclusive insurance from 2022 and added to the 2025 Commercial Insurance Innovative Drug Catalog

timeline.status: Established April 2017 in Shanghai's Zhangjiang Hi-Tech Park as a 50/50 JV between Fosun Pharma and Kite Pharma (US$20M from each side); chartered to develop, manufacture, and commercialize axicabtagene ciloleucel in China with options on additional Kite TCR programs
timeline.note: JV vehicle for Kite's Yescarta in China; 10,000 m² commercial manufacturing site + 2,000 m² R&D facility built in Zhangjiang Innovative Medicine Industry Park

timeline.status: Bridging Phase II trial of FKC876 (axicabtagene ciloleucel) in r/r large B-cell lymphoma; Yescarta technology transfer and local viral-vector and manufacturing infrastructure stood up to a Chinese GMP standard
timeline.note: FDA-approved CAR-T product first manufactured in China in 2018 to enable a domestic regulatory path

timeline.status: FKC876 (axicabtagene ciloleucel, brand name Yikaida 奕凯达 / Akilun) approved by NMPA on 23 June 2021 for adult r/r large B-cell lymphoma — China's first approved CAR-T cell therapy; bridging trial showed 79.2% response rate, in line with global Yescarta data
timeline.note: Listed at ~RMB 1.2M per infusion at launch; included in Shanghai 'Hu Hui Bao' inclusive insurance from 2022 to ease patient access

timeline.status: Yikaida second-line LBCL indication submitted (Oct 2022); Brexucabtagene autoleucel (Tecartus localized version) NMPA registration application accepted; Sept 13, 2024: Fosun Pharma agreed to acquire Kite/Gilead's 50% JV stake for ~US$27M plus a US$10M follow-on capital injection, citing Chinese reimbursement constraints; entity rebranded as Fosun Kairos
timeline.note: Kite retained the Yescarta and Tecartus license for China and continues to supply viral vectors; the JV exit is the most public early example of a multinational walking away from China cell-therapy economics under NHSA pricing

timeline.status: Yikaida (axicabtagene ciloleucel) listed on the inaugural 2025 Commercial Insurance Innovative Drug Catalog, broadening reimbursement beyond NRDL constraints; now a wholly-owned Fosun Pharma subsidiary under the Fosun Kairos name; pipeline expanding beyond Kite-licensed assets
timeline.note: Fosun Pharma 2025 group revenue RMB 41.66B (+1.5% YoY); innovative-drug revenue RMB 9.89B (+29.6%) with cell therapy contributing

milestones.label: Founded as 50/50 Fosun Pharma–Kite Pharma JV in Shanghai (April)
Yikaida (axicabtagene ciloleucel) — China's first approved CAR-T (NMPA, 23 June)
Yikaida added to Shanghai 'Hu Hui Bao' inclusive insurance; second-line LBCL filing accepted
Kite/Gilead exits the JV; Fosun Pharma acquires 50% stake for ~US$27M, rebrands as Fosun Kairos (Sept)
Yikaida listed in inaugural Commercial Insurance Innovative Drug Catalog

name: MGI Tech
nameZh: 深圳华大智造科技股份有限公司
shortDescription: BGI's sequencing-instrument arm, spun out in 2016 and STAR-listed in 2022; the only non-Western company shipping clinical NGS instruments at scale, and a recurring named target in US biosecurity legislation.
signature: DNBSEQ instrument line (T7+, T20, T1+, G99, E25)
narrativeHook: MGI Tech is what happened when Beijing Genomics Institute decided not to keep buying Illumina forever, bought Complete Genomics out of bankruptcy in 2013, spun the IP into its own company in 2016, and used the resulting DNBSEQ line to capture ~70% of Chinese sequencer tenders by 2025 — landing on the Pentagon's 1260H list and the BIOSECURE Act in the process.

leadAsset.name: DNBSEQ-T7+ / DNBSEQ-T20 / DNBSEQ-T1+
leadAsset.indication: Clinical and research genomics; population sequencing; oncology and reproductive testing
leadAsset.status: Roughly 70% domestic market share in China for sequencer tenders by 2025; T20 reads ~50,000 human genomes per year at ~$100/genome; T1+ launched March 2025; E25 cleared by NMPA and entered Europe and Latin America in 2025

timeline.status: Established 13 April 2016 in Shenzhen as a wholly owned BGI subsidiary to commercialize the DNB sequencing IP that BGI had acquired with Complete Genomics in 2013, and to break China's dependence on Illumina HiSeq instruments
timeline.note: BGI chairman Wang Jian retained chairmanship; the spinoff separated instrument economics from the BGI Genomics services business

timeline.status: Complete Genomics formally transferred from BGI Group to MGI Tech in 2018, putting San Jose-based DNB IP and US R&D headcount under MGI; first DNBSEQ-G400 deployments scaling outside China
timeline.note: Set up a dual operating footprint that became central to the later Illumina patent fight

timeline.status: May 2020 Series B raise of ~US$1B at a ~US$3B valuation, one of the largest private genomics rounds globally; IPO application submitted to the Shanghai STAR Market in December 2020
timeline.note: COVID-19 sequencing demand validated the instrument economics and pulled forward the public-listing path

timeline.status: STAR Market IPO on 9 September 2022 (688114.SH) raised RMB ~3.60B (~US$521M) at RMB 87.18/share; July 2022 settlement with Illumina ended multi-front US patent litigation, with Illumina paying US$325M to Complete Genomics and granting MGI a clear path to sell DNBSEQ products in the US after January 2023
timeline.note: May 2022 jury verdict separately awarded $333.8M to MGI's Complete Genomics on a two-channel sequencing patent claim against Illumina, before the global settlement subsumed it

timeline.status: Stock lost more than a third of its market value through 2024 as the BIOSECURE Act named MGI alongside BGI, MGI's affiliates and WuXi as 'biotechnology companies of concern'; opened Brazil and Latin America commercial operations and continued international DNBSEQ-T7+ rollout
timeline.note: FY2024 revenue ~RMB 2.59B with continuing net loss; pivot from China-only to a 'sovereign-genomics' export pitch in markets uncomfortable depending on Illumina

timeline.status: MGI and BGI placed on the 2025 Pentagon 1260H list of Chinese military companies; BIOSECURE Act signed into law on 17 December 2025 with MGI captured via the 1260H route. FY2025 revenue RMB 2.78B (+~7% YoY), domestic share ~70%, net loss narrowed by over 60% YoY; T1+ launched March 2025 with rapid installation growth, T7+ improved on cost and quality, E25 received NMPA clearance and entered EU and Latin American markets
timeline.note: Market cap ~US$4B as of late 2025; Complete Genomics, the US subsidiary, was not added to the 2025 1260H list, preserving a partial US commercial channel

milestones.label: Founded as a BGI subsidiary in Shenzhen (April 13)
Complete Genomics transferred from BGI Group to MGI Tech, anchoring US R&D and DNB IP
$1B Series B financing (May)
STAR Market IPO (688114) raises ~RMB 3.6B; Illumina settles for $325M to Complete Genomics, ending US patent litigation
Named in BIOSECURE Act bill text alongside BGI; market cap falls more than a third
Added to Pentagon 1260H list (Chinese military companies); BIOSECURE Act signed into law

name: Aiolos Bio
founders: Atlas Venture and Forbion (lead Series A investors)
shortDescription: US-domiciled NewCo founded by Atlas Venture and Forbion in late 2023 to develop AIO-001, an anti-TSLP monoclonal antibody for severe asthma licensed from Jiangsu Hengrui; acquired by GSK thirteen months later for up to $1.4 billion. Set the canonical template for the Chinese-asset-into-US-biotech NewCo structure.
narrativeHook: Aiolos was the moment the NewCo became real. A drug discovered at Jiangsu Hengrui was wrapped in a US-incorporated shell, funded with a $245M Series A from American venture capital, and acquired by GSK thirteen months later, proving the structure could turn a Chinese clinical asset into a $1.4 billion American exit while sidestepping BIOSECURE-shaped procurement friction.

leadAsset.name: AIO-001 / HRS-9821 (anti-TSLP monoclonal antibody)
leadAsset.indication: Severe asthma; potential expansion to other Th2-driven inflammatory diseases
leadAsset.status: Phase 2 development under GSK; carried forward as part of the Aiolos acquisition

milestones.label: Founded by Atlas Venture and Forbion (October); $245M Series A; AIO-001 licensed from Jiangsu Hengrui (ex-Greater China rights to HRS-9821)
Acquired by GSK for $1B upfront + up to $400M milestones (January 9, 2024)
